summary: A superb matrix recording captures the Oct 10 1980 show, highlighted by a flawless acoustic set and fire‑filled electric performances such as Althea, Jack Straw, Franklin’s Tower and Samson.
review: The Chappell matrix source delivers crystal‑clear sound that lets listeners hear every nuance of what many reviewers call one of the best 1980 shows.  The acoustic set is praised as a perfect prelude, while the electric sets shine with tight playing and standout renditions of Althea, Jack Straw, Franklin’s Tower, Samson and Candyman.  A few technical notes mention minor pitch‑correction issues, but overall the recording and performance are rated exceptionally high.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen enthusiastic reviews (mostly 5‑star) highlight both superb musical performance and excellent matrix sound quality; only minor technical criticisms appear, keeping the overall rating in the high‑range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix mix dominates with crisp, balanced sound; a few reviewers note pitch‑correction quirks and occasional dead‑air buzz between songs, but overall the audio is clear and well‑balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Acoustic set praised as a perfect warm‑up; electric sets flow tightly with a long, smoking second set and an energetic third set that maintains momentum despite the marathon length.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for restrained yet powerful playing and standout solos throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as great; his harmonies noted especially on acoustic numbers.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work is highlighted—both his tasteful “tinkling” and a few moments where his preset was critiqued.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming described as solid, thick and driving, particularly in the third set.
